title: Satyameva Jayate

infobox.value: सत्यमेव जयते  ([IAST](/wiki/International_Alphabet_of_Sanskrit_Transliteration "International Alphabet of Sanskrit Transliteration"): *Satyameva jayate*)

* [Flag of India](/wiki/Flag_of_India "Flag of India") (Flag) * [Emblem of India](/wiki/State_Emblem_of_India "State Emblem of India") (Emblem) * Satyameva Jayate (Motto) * [Jana Gana Mana](/wiki/Jana_Gana_Mana "Jana Gana Mana") (Anthem) * [Vande Mataram](/wiki/Vande_Mataram "Vande Mataram") (Song) * [National Pledge](/wiki/National_Pledge_(India) "National Pledge (India)") (Pledge) |

* [Bengal tiger](/wiki/Bengal_tiger "Bengal tiger") (Animal) * [Indian peafowl](/wiki/Indian_peafowl "Indian peafowl") (Bird) * [Lotus](/wiki/Nelumbo_nucifera "Nelumbo nucifera") (Flower) * [Banyan](/wiki/Ficus_benghalensis "Ficus benghalensis") (Tree) * [Mango](/wiki/Mangifera_indica "Mangifera indica") (Fruit) * [Indian elephant](/wiki/Indian_elephant "Indian elephant") (Heritage animal) * [Ganges river dolphin](/wiki/Ganges_river_dolphin "Ganges river dolphin") (Aquatic animal) * [King cobra](/wiki/King_cobra "King cobra") (Reptile) * [Lactobacillus bulgaricus](/wiki/Lactobacillus_delbrueckii_subsp._bulgaricus "Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. bulgaricus") (Microbe)

* [Independence Day](/wiki/Independence_Day_(India) "Independence Day (India)") * [Republic Day](/wiki/Republic_Day_(India) "Republic Day (India)") * [Gandhi Jayanti](/wiki/Gandhi_Jayanti "Gandhi Jayanti")

|  |  | | --- | --- | | [Symbols](/wiki/List_of_Uttarakhand_state_symbols "List of Uttarakhand state symbols") | * [Day](/wiki/Uttarakhand_Day "Uttarakhand Day") * [Emblem](/wiki/Emblem_of_Uttarakhand "Emblem of Uttarakhand") * [Song](/wiki/Uttarakhand_Devabhumi_Matribhumi "Uttarakhand Devabhumi Matribhumi") * Motto | | Executive | * [Governor](/wiki/List_of_governors_of_Uttarakhand "List of governors of Uttarakhand") * [Chief Minister](/wiki/List_of_chief_ministers_of_Uttarakhand "List of chief ministers of Uttarakhand") * [Council of Ministers](/wiki/Uttarakhand_Council_of_Ministers "Uttarakhand Council of Ministers")   + [Cabinets](/wiki/List_of_Uttarakhand_ministries "List of Uttarakhand ministries") * [Chief Secretary](/wiki/List_of_chief_secretaries_of_Uttarakhand "List of chief secretaries of Uttarakhand") * [Gazette](/wiki/Uttarakhand_Gazette "Uttarakhand Gazette") * [Agencies](/wiki/List_of_agencies_of_the_government_of_Uttarakhand "List of

([Sanskrit](/wiki/Sanskrit_language "Sanskrit language") pronunciation: [[sɐt̪jɐmeːʋɐ d͡ʒɐjɐt̪eː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Sanskrit "Help:IPA/Sanskrit"); lit. '**Truth Alone Triumphs**') is a part of a *[mantra](/wiki/Mantra "Mantra")* from the [Hindu scripture](/wiki/Hindu_texts "Hindu texts") *[Mundaka Upanishad](/wiki/Mundaka_Upanishad "Mundaka Upanishad")*.[[1]](#cite_note-iitk-1) Following the [independence](/wiki/Independence "Independence") of [India](/wiki/India "India"), it was adopted as the [national motto](/wiki/National_motto "National motto") of India on 26 January 1950, the day India became a [republic](/wiki/Republic_Day_(India) "Republic Day (India)").[[2]](#cite_note-Motto-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

["Motto for State Emblem"](http://pib.nic.in/archive/docs/DVD_38/ACC%20NO%20806-BR/HOM-1949-05-30_393.pdf) (PDF). *Press Information Bureau of India–Archive*. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20170808194446/http://pib.nic.in/archive/docs/DVD_38/ACC%20NO%20806-BR/HOM-1949-05-30_393.pdf) (PDF) from the original on 8 August 2017. Retrieved 8 August 2017.

["One hundred and sixteenth report on the state emblem of India (Prohibition of improper use) Bill, 2004"](http://164.100.47.5/book2/reports/home_aff/116threport.htm). *Government of India*. New Delhi: Rajya Sabha Secretariat: 6.11.1. 25 August 2005. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20110703134243/http://164.100.47.5/book2/reports/home_aff/116threport.htm) from the original on 3 July 2011. Retrieved 26 September 2008.

["The Mundaka Upanishad with Shankara's Commentary"](https://www.wisdomlib.org/hinduism/book/mundaka-upanishad-shankara-bhashya/d/doc145127.html). Wisdom Library. 21 February 2016. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20200626171356/https://www.wisdomlib.org/hinduism/book/mundaka-upanishad-shankara-bhashya/d/doc145127.html) from the original on 26 June 2020. Retrieved 12 December 2017.

Swami Krishnananda. ["The Mundaka Upanishad:Third Mundaka, First Khanda"](http://www.swami-krishnananda.org/mundak/mun_3-1.html). *swami-krishnananda.org*. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20101221053512/http://swami-krishnananda.org/mundak/mun_3-1.html) from the original on 21 December 2010. Retrieved 8 September 2014.

["Minutes of the first meeting of the National Committee for Commemoration of 150th Birth Anniversary of Mahamana Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya 26 July 2011 at 6.00 pm–7, Race Course Road, New Delhi"](http://indiaculture.nic.in/indiaculture/pdf/Final%20Minutes_MMM-26july,%202011.pdf) (PDF). [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20130313071122/http://indiaculture.nic.in/indiaculture/pdf/Final%20Minutes_MMM-26july,%202011.pdf) (PDF) from the original on 13 March 2013. Retrieved 8 September 2014.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

National motto of India

For other uses, see [Satyamev Jayate (disambiguation)](/wiki/Satyamev_Jayate_(disambiguation) "Satyamev Jayate (disambiguation)").

| Translations of *Satyameva jayate* | |
| --- | --- |
| English | **Truth Alone Triumphs** |
| [Sanskrit](/wiki/Sanskrit "Sanskrit") | सत्यमेव जयते  ([IAST](/wiki/International_Alphabet_of_Sanskrit_Transliteration "International Alphabet of Sanskrit Transliteration"): *Satyameva jayate*) |
| [Assamese](/wiki/Assamese_language "Assamese language") | সত্যমেৱ জয়তে |
| [Bengali](/wiki/Bengali_language "Bengali language") | সত্যমেব জয়তে |
| [Bhojpuri](/wiki/Bhojpuri_language "Bhojpuri language") | 𑂮𑂞𑂹𑂨𑂧𑂵𑂫 𑂔𑂨𑂞𑂵 |
| [Hindi](/wiki/Hindi "Hindi") | सत्यमेव जयते |
| [Kannada](/wiki/Kannada "Kannada") | ಸತ್ಯಮೇವ ಜಯತೇ |

| [Glossary of Hinduism terms](/wiki/Glossary_of_Hinduism_terms "Glossary of Hinduism terms") | |

**Satyameva Jayate** ([Sanskrit](/wiki/Sanskrit_language "Sanskrit language") pronunciation: [[sɐt̪jɐmeːʋɐ d͡ʒɐjɐt̪eː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Sanskrit "Help:IPA/Sanskrit"); lit. '**Truth Alone Triumphs**') is a part of a *[mantra](/wiki/Mantra "Mantra")* from the [Hindu scripture](/wiki/Hindu_texts "Hindu texts") *[Mundaka Upanishad](/wiki/Mundaka_Upanishad "Mundaka Upanishad")*.[[1]](#cite_note-iitk-1) Following the [independence](/wiki/Independence "Independence") of [India](/wiki/India "India"), it was adopted as the [national motto](/wiki/National_motto "National motto") of India on 26 January 1950, the day India became a [republic](/wiki/Republic_Day_(India) "Republic Day (India)").[[2]](#cite_note-Motto-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)
In the [national emblem of India](/wiki/State_Emblem_of_India "State Emblem of India"), it is inscribed in the [Devanagari](/wiki/Devanagari "Devanagari") script below the [Lion Capital of Ashoka](/wiki/Lion_Capital_of_Ashoka "Lion Capital of Ashoka") and forms an integral part of the emblem. The emblem, including "Satyameva Jayate", is inscribed on one side of all [Indian currency](/wiki/Indian_currency "Indian currency") and national documents.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/55/Emblem_of_India.svg/250px-Emblem_of_India.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Emblem_of_India.svg)

[State Emblem of India](/wiki/State_Emblem_of_India "State Emblem of India") contains the phrase *Satyameva Jayate*.

## Origin

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Satyameva_Jayate&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Origin")]

The origin of the [motto](/wiki/Motto "Motto") is the [mantra](/wiki/Mantra "Mantra") 3.1.6 from the *[Mundaka Upanishad](/wiki/Mundaka_Upanishad "Mundaka Upanishad")*, which reads:

In the Devanāgarī script

> **सत्यमेव जयते** नानृतं सत्येन पन्था विततो देवयानः।  
> येनाक्रमन्त्यृषयो ह्याप्तकामा यत्र तत् सत्यस्य परमं निधानम्॥[[1]](#cite_note-iitk-1)

Transliteration

> ***satyameva jayate** nānṛtaṃ*  
> *satyena panthā vitato devayānaḥ*  
> *yenākramantyṛṣayo hyāptakāmā*  
> *yatra tat satyasya paramaṃ nidhānam*[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In English

> **Truth Alone Triumphs**; not falsehood.  
> Through truth the divine path is spread out  
> by which the sages whose desires have been completely fulfilled,  
> reach to where is that supreme treasure of Truth.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The phrase is composed of the words *[satyam](/wiki/Satya "Satya")* ("truth"), *[eva](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%A4%8F%E0%A4%B5 "wikt:एव")* (emphatic particle, ~"indeed"), and [jayate](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%A4%9C%E0%A4%AF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%87 "wikt:जयते") ("conquers").

## Popular connotations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Satyameva_Jayate&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Popular connotations")]

Popular connotations also include:

* 'Truth stands Invincible'
* 'Truth alone conquers, not falsehood'
* 'The true prevails, not the untrue'[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* 'Veritas Vincit', a direct Latin translation.
* 'Truth alone conquers, not untruth'[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* 'Truth Alone Triumphs, not that against Sacred law ([Rta](/wiki/Rta "Rta"))
* Vaymaiye Vellum ([Tamil](/wiki/Tamil_language "Tamil language"): வாய்மையே வெல்லும்)

The slogan was popularized and brought into the national lexicon by [Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya](/wiki/Madan_Mohan_Malaviya "Madan Mohan Malaviya") in 1918 when serving his second of four terms as president of the [Indian National Congress](/wiki/Indian_National_Congress "Indian National Congress").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Satyameva_Jayate&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Veritas vincit](/wiki/Veritas_vincit "Veritas vincit"), the national motto of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic") that has the same meaning.
